North Beach Branch Library Mary Chou Action Ms. Chou presented the revised design by Bill Fontana for the sound installation at the North Beach Branch Library. Ms. Chou reported that the artist was in the Design Development Phase, and through this process discovered the need to pare dow n his design to stay within the project budget. Ms. Chou explained that originally the artist had 16 speakers included in his design which will now be reduced to eight.

ocr_excerpts: of the Library group will be ordan- ized on a permanent basis at an organizational meeting to be held during January 1955* Commissioner Q,uan referred to a Committee meeting held prior to the regular meeting of the Commission and described in detail proposed alterations and additions to the present North Beach Branch Library. Commissioner Q,uan concluded his report by stating that additional information will be for thcoming at the next regular meeting of the Commission. From: Norman F. G-runke, Executor, Estate of Rene Sarazen re: Bequest of decedent's musical library to the San Francisco Pub. Library. Acknowledge and r

75.00 100.00 100.00 $ 105, 723.91 189,545.80 LIBRARY OPERATIONS COMMITTEE Commissioner La Place informed the Commission of a communica- tion from Mr. Stanton Jue, Director of the International Institute of San Francisco, addressed to Commissioner Quan, relative to additional hours of service at the North Beach Branch Library. Commissioner La Place recommended that additional hours of service as requested be granted and referred this matter to the City Librarian for his deliberation and recommendation. It was so recommended. WELFARE OF EMPLOYEES COMMITTEE Commissioner Dettmering moved and Commissioner Fischer seconded that

Commission, this request be regretfully denied. Commissioner Fischer concluded his report by informing the Commission of a communication received from Mr. Stanton Jue, Director of the International Institute of San Francisco, thanking the Commission and Librarian for extension of hours of service at the North Beach Branch Library. The Chair directed the Secretary to acknowledge this communication. FINANCE, AUDIT AND BUDGET COMMITTEE Commissioner Fischer moved, and Commissioner Dettmering seoonded that the Commission approve the following list of expenditures for March and estimated expenditures for April: APR 1 2 1955 454 E

ntioned resolution, Commissioner Mohr be designated as the official delegate from the Library Commission to the ALA Conference in Philadelphia. It was so ordered. CHINATOWN LIBRARY COMMITTEE Commissioner Q,uan referred to a communication from the Chinese Six Companies relative to the conversion of the North Beach Branch Library to a Chinatown Branch Library, and their expressed enthusiasm and support for this project. Commissioner Q,uan stated that plans have been formulated lor a. Citizens' Committee of outstanding Chinese and Americans to assist in raising funds to reconstruct the present outmoded premises. hs The schedule for each individual project conforms to the program phasing criteria listed on page 3. The criteria balance the relative priority of each project with the need to maintain library services in each neighborhood during the life of the program. 51 Branch Library Improvement Program NORTH BEACH BRANCH LIBRARY BACKGROUND The North Beach Branch Library, opened to the public in 1959, is a 5,097 square foot building with an irregular plan configuration, is located at 2000 Mason Street, adjacent to a Recreation and Park facility. It serves North Beach, Telegraph Hill, the Financial District, and a portion of the Chinatown- District. Library services a

n program excludes library staff costs. The solution to be chosen for a particular branch will depend on many factors involving overall program phasing, the extent of disruptions, and the cost effectiveness of the various relocation alternatives. 53 Branch Library Improvement Program PROJECT COST North Beach Branch Library Project Cost Summary Rehabilitation and Improvements $3,190,000 Expansion $500,000 Total Project Cost Estimate $3,690,000 Project costs include construction cost estimates for each project component: principal construction; construction contingency; art enrichment; hazardous materials mitigation; temporary relocation measures; project control and site control; cost figures are escalated to the mid-point of construction. PROJECT SCHEDULE North Beach Branch Library Project Duration ACTIVITY DURATION Planning Design and Bid 26 months Relocation and Construction 19 months The schedule for each individual project conforms to the program phasing criteria listed on page 3.
